projectDescription: A Flask-based web application for real-time meteorological monitoring in Madrid, integrating AEMET OpenData to provide weather data, manual data logging, historical queries, and comparative analysis.
keyFeatures: Real-time weather data via AEMET OpenData API with GPS-based nearest station detection using the Haversine formula
Manual climate data logging with validation and dual persistence (JSON files + SQLite database)
Historical data query and filtering by municipality and date
Comparative analysis between user-recorded data and official AEMET readings
Automated alert system with color-coded severity levels (red, orange, frost, high wind, heavy rain, high humidity)
Scheduled background data fetching with retry logic for resilient API communication
User authentication system with session management and password hashing
publicApiSurface: GET /api/clima?lat=&lon= — Fetch real-time weather for coordinates
POST /api/registrar — Persist a new climate record
GET/POST /consulta — Query and filter historical climate records
GET/POST /comparar — Compare manual records against AEMET data
